items.text: The campus community garden is finally open for spring! Plots are available every day from 8 AM to 6 PM near Miller Hall. Grab a plot for just $5 per season and start planting. Tools are free to borrow at the shed. So excited!

questions.stem: According to the post, how much does a garden plot cost?
questions.explanation: The post states plots are 'just $5 per season,' which matches five dollars each season.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about gardeners who need tools?

options.A: They must purchase their own tools
options.B: They do not need to bring their own tools
options.C: They pay a deposit for the tools
options.D: They can only use tools on weekends

questions.explanation: Since tools are free to borrow at the shed, gardeners do not need to supply their own.

items.text: Hi Marcus, Thanks for signing up to volunteer as a campus tour guide. Your shift is confirmed for Saturday, April 18, from 10 AM to noon. Please meet at the Visitor Center by 9:45 to pick up your badge. See you then! Best, Dana

questions.stem: According to the email, what should Marcus do at 9:45?
questions.explanation: The email asks Marcus to meet by 9:45 to pick up his badge, meaning he should collect it.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about when Marcus should arrive?

options.B: He should come exactly at noon
options.C: He can arrive any time before noon
options.D: He should arrive before his shift begins

questions.explanation: He is told to meet by 9:45 while the shift starts at 10 AM, so he should arrive before the shift begins.

items.text: Just tried the new lavender latte at Bean Scene café in the student union — so good! It's only $4.50 and they're serving it all week until Friday. Get there before 11 AM to skip the morning rush. Honestly the best drink on campus!

questions.stem: According to the post, where is the lavender latte sold?

options.A: At a café in the union
options.B: At a stand by the library
options.D: At a food cart near the gym

questions.explanation: The post says the drink is served at Bean Scene café in the student union.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about visiting the café after 11 AM?

options.A: The latte will be sold out
options.B: The café will be busier then
options.C: The price will be higher then

questions.explanation: Arriving before 11 AM is recommended to skip the morning rush, implying the café is busier afterward.

items.text: Dear Priya, Good news — the title you ordered via interlibrary loan is now waiting for you at the Circulation Desk. Please collect it by Tuesday, May 12, and bring $2 for the handling fee. Return it within three weeks. Thank you, Reference Services

questions.stem: According to the email, how much should Priya bring?
questions.explanation: The email asks Priya to bring $2 for the handling fee.

questions.stem: According to the email, how long may Priya keep the book?
questions.explanation: The email states the book must be returned within three weeks.
